text: Ever wonder why we only see one side of the Moon? It's not hiding. The Moon is tidally locked, spinning in perfect sync with its orbit around Earth. This means it takes the same time to rotate once as it does to orbit Earth — about 27.3 days. So, while it may seem like the Moon's playing coy, it's just how physics works! This tidal locking creates phases, making our Moon's appearance change over time. Want to learn more cool science facts? Hit that follow button!
